evals.prompt: nvimの設定ファイルを更新したんでコミットして
evals.expected_output: Conventional Commits subject line with feat or chore type, nvim scope. Simple change so no body needed.
assertions: Subject line follows Conventional Commits format: <type>[optional scope]: <description>
Subject line is 72 characters or fewer
Type is appropriate for a feature addition (feat or chore)
[with_skill only] Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com> is present in the commit

evals.prompt: LSP関連の設定のバグを直したのでコミットしたい
evals.expected_output: fix type commit with nvim scope, body explaining the deprecated API migration from formatting_sync to format
assertions: Subject line follows Conventional Commits format: <type>[optional scope]: <description>
Subject line is 72 characters or fewer
Type is 'fix' (it's a bug fix for deprecated API usage)
A commit body is present explaining what was fixed
[with_skill only] Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com> is present in the commit

evals.prompt: 今日書いたコード全部コミットしたい。nvimとpowershellの設定を両方いじってた
evals.expected_output: Commit covers both nvim and powershell changes. Skill should flag mixed-area files before staging.
assertions: Subject line follows Conventional Commits format: <type>[optional scope]: <description>
Subject line is 72 characters or fewer
Commit message references or covers both changed areas (nvim and powershell)
[with_skill only] Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com> is present in the commit
